exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def create_density_matrix(states: list, probabilities: list) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Create density matrix ρ = Σᵢ pᵢ |ψᵢ⟩⟨ψᵢ|.
    """
    dim = states[0].shape[0]
    rho = np.zeros((dim, dim), dtype=complex)
    
    for state, prob in zip(states, probabilities):
        # Normalize state if needed
        state = state / np.linalg.norm(state)
        rho += prob * (state @ state.conj().T)
    
    return rho

def is_valid_density_matrix(rho: np.ndarray, tol: float = 1e-10) -> dict:
    """
    Check if matrix is a valid density matrix.
    """
    # Hermitian check
    hermitian = np.allclose(rho, rho.conj().T, atol=tol)
    
    # Positive semidefinite check (all eigenvalues >= 0)
    eigenvalues = np.linalg.eigvalsh(rho)
    positive = np.all(eigenvalues >= -tol)
    
    # Unit trace check
    unit_trace = np.isclose(np.trace(rho), 1, atol=tol)
    
    return {
        'hermitian': hermitian,
        'positive': positive,
        'unit_trace': unit_trace,
        'valid': hermitian and positive and unit_trace
    }

def purity(rho: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """Calculate purity Tr(ρ²)."""
    return np.real(np.trace(rho @ rho))

def is_pure_state(rho: np.ndarray, tol: float = 1e-10) -> bool:
    """Check if density matrix represents a pure state."""
    return np.isclose(purity(rho), 1, atol=tol)

# Test
ket_0 = np.array([[1], [0]], dtype=complex)
ket_1 = np.array([[0], [1]], dtype=complex)
ket_plus = np.array([[1], [1]], dtype=complex) / np.sqrt(2)

print("Pure state |0⟩:")
rho_0 = create_density_matrix([ket_0], [1.0])
print(rho_0)
print(f"Valid: {is_valid_density_matrix(rho_0)}")
print(f"Purity: {purity(rho_0):.4f}")
print(f"Is pure: {is_pure_state(rho_0)}")

print("\nPure state |+⟩:")
rho_plus = create_density_matrix([ket_plus], [1.0])
print(np.round(rho_plus, 4))
print(f"Purity: {purity(rho_plus):.4f}")
print(f"Is pure: {is_pure_state(rho_plus)}")

print("\n50-50 mixed state:")
rho_mixed = create_density_matrix([ket_0, ket_1], [0.5, 0.5])
print(rho_mixed)
print(f"Purity: {purity(rho_mixed):.4f}")
print(f"Is pure: {is_pure_state(rho_mixed)}")

print("\n70-30 mixed state:")
rho_70_30 = create_density_matrix([ket_0, ket_1], [0.7, 0.3])
print(rho_70_30)
print(f"Purity: {purity(rho_70_30):.4f}")
hints: ρ = Σᵢ pᵢ |ψᵢ⟩⟨ψᵢ| = Σᵢ pᵢ (state @ state.conj().T)

exercises.title: Von Neumann Entropy Calculator
exercises.description: Implement von Neumann entropy and analyze quantum vs classical correlations.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def von_neumann_entropy(rho: np.ndarray) -> float:

    Calculate S(ρ) = -Tr(ρ log₂ ρ) = -Σᵢ λᵢ log₂(λᵢ).

def mutual_information(rho_AB: np.ndarray, dim_A: int, dim_B: int) -> float:
    """
    Calculate I(A:B) = S(ρ_A) + S(ρ_B) - S(ρ_AB).
    """
    # Your code here

# Test
ket_0 = np.array([[1], [0]], dtype=complex)
rho_pure = ket_0 @ ket_0.conj().T
rho_max_mixed = np.eye(2, dtype=complex) / 2

print("Entropy of pure state:", von_neumann_entropy(rho_pure))
print("Entropy of maximally mixed:", von_neumann_entropy(rho_max_mixed))
print(f"Maximum possible (d=2): {np.log2(2):.4f}")

# Bell state
ket_00 = np.array([[1], [0], [0], [0]], dtype=complex)
ket_11 = np.array([[0], [0], [0], [1]], dtype=complex)
bell = (ket_00 + ket_11) / np.sqrt(2)
rho_bell = bell @ bell.conj().T

print(f"\nBell state mutual information: {mutual_information(rho_bell, 2, 2):.4f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def von_neumann_entropy(rho: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """
    Calculate S(ρ) = -Tr(ρ log₂ ρ).
    """
    eigenvalues = np.linalg.eigvalsh(rho)
    # Filter out zero/negative eigenvalues (numerical noise)
    eigenvalues = eigenvalues[eigenvalues > 1e-15]
    return -np.sum(eigenvalues * np.log2(eigenvalues))

def partial_trace_B(rho_AB: np.ndarray, dim_A: int, dim_B: int) -> np.ndarray:
    """Trace out system B."""
    reshaped = rho_AB.reshape(dim_A, dim_B, dim_A, dim_B)
    return np.trace(reshaped, axis1=1, axis2=3)

def partial_trace_A(rho_AB: np.ndarray, dim_A: int, dim_B: int) -> np.ndarray:
    """Trace out system A."""
    reshaped = rho_AB.reshape(dim_A, dim_B, dim_A, dim_B)
    return np.trace(reshaped, axis1=0, axis2=2)

def relative_entropy(rho: np.ndarray, sigma: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """
    Calculate relative entropy S(ρ||σ).
    """
    # Get eigendecomposition
    ev_rho, U_rho = np.linalg.eigh(rho)
    ev_sigma, U_sigma = np.linalg.eigh(sigma)
    
    # Filter small eigenvalues
    mask_rho = ev_rho > 1e-15
    mask_sigma = ev_sigma > 1e-15
    
    # S(ρ||σ) = Tr(ρ log ρ) - Tr(ρ log σ)
    # = -S(ρ) - Tr(ρ log σ)
    S_rho = von_neumann_entropy(rho)
    
    # Calculate Tr(ρ log σ) via eigendecomposition
    log_sigma = U_sigma @ np.diag(np.where(ev_sigma > 1e-15, np.log2(ev_sigma), 0)) @ U_sigma.conj().T
    tr_rho_log_sigma = np.real(np.trace(rho @ log_sigma))
    
    return -S_rho - tr_rho_log_sigma

def mutual_information(rho_AB: np.ndarray, dim_A: int, dim_B: int) -> float:
    """
    Calculate I(A:B) = S(ρ_A) + S(ρ_B) - S(ρ_AB).
    """
    rho_A = partial_trace_B(rho_AB, dim_A, dim_B)
    rho_B = partial_trace_A(rho_AB, dim_A, dim_B)
    
    S_A = von_neumann_entropy(rho_A)
    S_B = von_neumann_entropy(rho_B)
    S_AB = von_neumann_entropy(rho_AB)
    
    return S_A + S_B - S_AB

# Test
ket_0 = np.array([[1], [0]], dtype=complex)
rho_pure = ket_0 @ ket_0.conj().T
rho_max_mixed = np.eye(2, dtype=complex) / 2

print("Von Neumann Entropy:")
print(f"  Pure state: {von_neumann_entropy(rho_pure):.4f}")
print(f"  Maximally mixed: {von_neumann_entropy(rho_max_mixed):.4f}")
print(f"  Maximum (d=2): {np.log2(2):.4f}")

# Partially mixed states
print("\nPartially mixed states:")
for p in [0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9]:
    rho = p * rho_pure + (1-p) * rho_max_mixed
    S = von_neumann_entropy(rho)
    print(f"  p={p}: S={S:.4f}")

# Bell state
ket_00 = np.array([[1], [0], [0], [0]], dtype=complex)
ket_11 = np.array([[0], [0], [0], [1]], dtype=complex)
bell = (ket_00 + ket_11) / np.sqrt(2)
rho_bell = bell @ bell.conj().T

print(f"\nBell state analysis:")
print(f"  S(ρ_AB) = {von_neumann_entropy(rho_bell):.4f} (pure)")
rho_A = partial_trace_B(rho_bell, 2, 2)
print(f"  S(ρ_A) = {von_neumann_entropy(rho_A):.4f} (max mixed)")
print(f"  I(A:B) = {mutual_information(rho_bell, 2, 2):.4f}")

# Product state comparison
ket_plus = np.array([[1], [1]], dtype=complex) / np.sqrt(2)
product = np.kron(ket_plus, ket_plus)
rho_product = product @ product.conj().T
print(f"\nProduct state |+⟩|+⟩:")
print(f"  I(A:B) = {mutual_information(rho_product, 2, 2):.4f} (no correlation)")
hints: Use partial traces to get reduced density matrices

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

# Pauli matrices
X = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]], dtype=complex)
Y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]])
Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]], dtype=complex)
I = np.eye(2, dtype=complex)

def bloch_vector(rho: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Extract Bloch vector from density matrix.
    """
    rx = np.real(np.trace(rho @ X))
    ry = np.real(np.trace(rho @ Y))
    rz = np.real(np.trace(rho @ Z))
    return np.array([rx, ry, rz])

def density_from_bloch(r: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Create density matrix from Bloch vector.
    """
    return 0.5 * (I + r[0]*X + r[1]*Y + r[2]*Z)

def is_physical_bloch(r: np.ndarray, tol: float = 1e-10) -> bool:
    """
    Check if Bloch vector corresponds to physical state.
    """
    return np.linalg.norm(r) <= 1 + tol

def purity_from_bloch(r: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """
    Calculate purity from Bloch vector.
    """
    return (1 + np.dot(r, r)) / 2

def entropy_from_bloch(r: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """
    Calculate entropy from Bloch vector.
    """
    r_mag = np.linalg.norm(r)
    if r_mag >= 1 - 1e-10:
        return 0  # Pure state
    
    # Eigenvalues are (1 ± |r|)/2
    lambda_plus = (1 + r_mag) / 2
    lambda_minus = (1 - r_mag) / 2
    
    S = 0
    if lambda_plus > 1e-15:
        S -= lambda_plus * np.log2(lambda_plus)
    if lambda_minus > 1e-15:
        S -= lambda_minus * np.log2(lambda_minus)
    
    return S

# Test
print("Standard states on Bloch sphere:")
states = {
    '|0⟩': np.array([[1, 0], [0, 0]], dtype=complex),
    '|1⟩': np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex),
    '|+⟩': np.array([[0.5, 0.5], [0.5, 0.5]], dtype=complex),
    '|-⟩': np.array([[0.5, -0.5], [-0.5, 0.5]], dtype=complex),
    '|+i⟩': np.array([[0.5, -0.5j], [0.5j, 0.5]], dtype=complex),
    'mixed': np.eye(2, dtype=complex) / 2
}

for name, rho in states.items():
    r = bloch_vector(rho)
    r_mag = np.linalg.norm(r)
    gamma = purity_from_bloch(r)
    print(f"  {name}: r = {np.round(r, 4)}, |r| = {r_mag:.4f}, purity = {gamma:.4f}")

# Verify round-trip
print("\nRound-trip verification:")
for name, rho in states.items():
    r = bloch_vector(rho)
    rho_reconstructed = density_from_bloch(r)
    match = np.allclose(rho, rho_reconstructed)
    print(f"  {name}: {match}")

# Test physical states
print("\nPhysical state check:")
test_vectors = [
    np.array([0, 0, 0]),      # Center
    np.array([0, 0, 1]),      # |0⟩
    np.array([1, 0, 0]),      # |+⟩
    np.array([0.5, 0.5, 0.5]),# Inside sphere
    np.array([1, 1, 0]),      # Outside! |r| = √2
]

for r in test_vectors:
    physical = is_physical_bloch(r)
    print(f"  r = {r}: |r| = {np.linalg.norm(r):.4f}, physical = {physical}")

# Entropy from Bloch vector
print("\nEntropy from Bloch representation:")
for r_mag in [0, 0.5, 0.9, 0.99, 1.0]:
    r = np.array([0, 0, r_mag])
    S = entropy_from_bloch(r)
    print(f"  |r| = {r_mag}: S = {S:.4f}")

exercises.title: Quantum State Tomography
exercises.description: Reconstruct a density matrix from simulated measurement outcomes.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def simulate_measurements(rho: np.ndarray, observable: np.ndarray, 
                          n_shots: int) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Simulate n_shots measurements of observable on state rho.
    Returns array of measurement outcomes.

    pass

def estimate_expectation(outcomes: np.ndarray, eigenvalues: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """
    Estimate expectation value from measurement outcomes.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def tomography_1qubit(rho: np.ndarray, n_shots: int = 1000) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Perform quantum state tomography on single qubit.
    Measure X, Y, Z and reconstruct density matrix.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def fidelity(rho: np.ndarray, sigma: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """
    Calculate fidelity F(ρ,σ) = Tr(√(√ρ σ √ρ))².
    For pure σ = |ψ⟩⟨ψ|: F = ⟨ψ|ρ|ψ⟩.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test
np.random.seed(42)

# Create a test state
ket_test = np.array([[np.cos(0.3)], [np.sin(0.3) * np.exp(1j * 0.7)]])
rho_true = ket_test @ ket_test.conj().T

print("True state:")
print(np.round(rho_true, 4))

rho_estimated = tomography_1qubit(rho_true, n_shots=10000)
print("\nEstimated state:")
print(np.round(rho_estimated, 4))

F = fidelity(rho_true, rho_estimated)
print(f"\nFidelity: {F:.4f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
from scipy.linalg import sqrtm

# Pauli matrices
X = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]], dtype=complex)
Y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]])
Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]], dtype=complex)
I = np.eye(2, dtype=complex)

def simulate_measurements(rho: np.ndarray, observable: np.ndarray, 
                          n_shots: int) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Simulate measurements of observable on state rho.
    """
    # Get eigendecomposition
    eigenvalues, eigenvectors = np.linalg.eigh(observable)
    
    # Calculate probabilities
    probs = []
    for i in range(len(eigenvalues)):
        v = eigenvectors[:, i:i+1]
        P = v @ v.conj().T  # Projector
        prob = np.real(np.trace(rho @ P))
        probs.append(max(0, prob))  # Ensure non-negative
    
    probs = np.array(probs)
    probs = probs / np.sum(probs)  # Normalize
    
    # Sample outcomes
    outcomes = np.random.choice(len(eigenvalues), size=n_shots, p=probs)
    return eigenvalues[outcomes]

def estimate_expectation(outcomes: np.ndarray, eigenvalues: np.ndarray = None) -> float:
    """
    Estimate expectation value from measurement outcomes.
    """
    return np.mean(outcomes)

def tomography_1qubit(rho: np.ndarray, n_shots: int = 1000) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Perform quantum state tomography on single qubit.
    """
    # Measure in X, Y, Z bases
    outcomes_X = simulate_measurements(rho, X, n_shots)
    outcomes_Y = simulate_measurements(rho, Y, n_shots)
    outcomes_Z = simulate_measurements(rho, Z, n_shots)
    
    # Estimate Bloch vector components
    rx = estimate_expectation(outcomes_X)
    ry = estimate_expectation(outcomes_Y)
    rz = estimate_expectation(outcomes_Z)
    
    # Reconstruct density matrix
    rho_est = 0.5 * (I + rx*X + ry*Y + rz*Z)
    
    # Ensure valid density matrix (project to physical)
    eigenvalues, eigenvectors = np.linalg.eigh(rho_est)
    eigenvalues = np.maximum(eigenvalues, 0)  # Make positive
    eigenvalues = eigenvalues / np.sum(eigenvalues)  # Normalize trace
    rho_est = eigenvectors @ np.diag(eigenvalues) @ eigenvectors.conj().T
    
    return rho_est

def fidelity(rho: np.ndarray, sigma: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """
    Calculate fidelity F(ρ,σ).
    """
    sqrt_rho = sqrtm(rho)
    inner = sqrtm(sqrt_rho @ sigma @ sqrt_rho)
    return np.real(np.trace(inner))**2

def fidelity_simple(rho: np.ndarray, sigma: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """
    Simplified fidelity for when one state is pure.
    """
    # Use F = Tr(ρσ) as approximation when states are close
    return np.real(np.trace(sqrtm(sqrtm(rho) @ sigma @ sqrtm(rho))))**2

# Test
np.random.seed(42)

# Create test states
test_states = {
    '|0⟩': np.array([[1], [0]], dtype=complex),
    '|+⟩': np.array([[1], [1]], dtype=complex) / np.sqrt(2),
    'rotated': np.array([[np.cos(0.3)], [np.sin(0.3) * np.exp(1j * 0.7)]])
}

for name, ket in test_states.items():
    rho_true = ket @ ket.conj().T
    
    print(f"\n=== {name} ===")
    print("True Bloch vector:", end=" ")
    r_true = np.array([np.real(np.trace(rho_true @ P)) for P in [X, Y, Z]])
    print(np.round(r_true, 4))
    
    # Tomography with different shot counts
    for n_shots in [100, 1000, 10000]:
        rho_est = tomography_1qubit(rho_true, n_shots)
        r_est = np.array([np.real(np.trace(rho_est @ P)) for P in [X, Y, Z]])
        F = fidelity(rho_true, rho_est)
        print(f"  {n_shots} shots: r_est = {np.round(r_est, 3)}, F = {F:.4f}")

# Mixed state tomography
print("\n=== Mixed state ===")
rho_mixed = 0.7 * (np.array([[1], [0]]) @ np.array([[1, 0]])) + \
            0.3 * (np.eye(2) / 2)
rho_mixed = rho_mixed.astype(complex)

print("True state:")
print(np.round(rho_mixed, 4))
print(f"True purity: {np.real(np.trace(rho_mixed @ rho_mixed)):.4f}")

rho_est = tomography_1qubit(rho_mixed, n_shots=10000)
print("\nEstimated state:")
print(np.round(rho_est, 4))
print(f"Estimated purity: {np.real(np.trace(rho_est @ rho_est)):.4f}")
print(f"Fidelity: {fidelity(rho_mixed, rho_est):.4f}")
hints: Measure X, Y, Z to get Bloch vector components
